other_observers: none; I believe that I was the original finder of this bird (it was independently observed by Dave Rintoul on Sept. 26).
specific_location: San Luis Lakes State Park, along the eastern shore of one of the lakes
description: I observed the bird as it was standing on the shore of a lake, with no other birds nearby.  It appeared to be a medium-sized gull (my size estimate was uncertain, with no other birds for comparison).  It had a completely gray (not black) hood, with no white. The hood came down only to the middle of the nape.  They eye was very inconspicuous (a dark eye on a dark head).  The bill was black, with a yellow tip (outer third of the bill).  The underparts were a clean white.  The back was a clean gray, about the shade of the back of an adult Ring-billed Gull.  The wing tips (seen when folded) were blackish, with three tiny white spots visible.  On the shaded (i.e. in shadow) side of the head, there appeared to be a narrow black line between the gray hood and the white neck. This feature was not apparent on the sunlit side.Behaviors: It was standing on the shoreline of a lake during my entire observation.Call: none heardPlumage: adult breeding plumage
